network.note_wifi_country: Bootstrap Wi-Fi regulatory country (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2). NOT a lock: the Pi's radio needs some valid country to start the first setup hotspot before the wizard runs (the 'world'/00 domain blocks AP mode). The user picks their own country in the wizard, which overwrites this and is applied via iw reg set + raspi-config. Build a regional image with IOTSENTINEL_WIFI_COUNTRY.
network.note_capture_mode: capture_mode: 'passive' (Zeek sniffs network.interface) or 'gateway' (Pi is a Wi-Fi AP on ap_interface via a USB dongle; all IoT traffic + DNS routed through the Pi for full visibility + inline enforcement). Home Wi-Fi (network.interface as client uplink) is never modified.

ml.note: River-based incremental learning - no training needed!

ai_assistant.note: API keys loaded from GROQ_API_KEY / OPENAI_API_KEY / ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / GEMINI_API_KEY environment variables

email.note: Email settings are loaded from environment variables (.env file)

notifications.note: Values loaded from NOTIFICATIONS_* environment variables (.env file)

agent.note: auto_block.enabled=true means the agent enforces firewall blocks autonomously on critical alerts. Set to false to revert to human-approval mode.

threat_intelligence.note: API key loaded from THREAT_INTELLIGENCE_ABUSEIPDB_API_KEY environment variable

nvd.note: API key loaded from NVD_API_KEY environment variable

demo.note_seed_traffic: DEMO BUILD ONLY: on first boot, inject sample anomaly traffic so the dashboard shows live alerts for the demonstration video, with zero manual steps. Set this to false for the public/production release so real installs are never seeded with synthetic traffic.

security.note_remember_cookie: How long 'Remember Me' keeps you signed in. Longer = fewer logins on the LAN-over-HTTP path where biometrics (which need HTTPS) cannot run.
security.note_https: Serve the dashboard over HTTPS with a self-signed cert (generated at boot in data/certs). Enables biometrics (Touch ID/Face ID) and the PWA on the LAN and encrypts password login. The browser shows a one-time 'not private' warning to accept (tap Advanced -> Proceed). If cert generation ever fails the dashboard falls back to plain HTTP automatically. Access via https://iotsentinel.local:8050. Set false (or env IOTSENTINEL_HTTPS=false) to force plain HTTP.
